---
title: "Data Filtration Overview"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Global chunk options
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  cache.path = "cache/",
  fig.retina = 2
)

# Reproducibility
set.seed(1)

############################################################
# 0) Clear R "brain" + libraries
############################################################
rm(list = ls())
gc()

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyr)
  library(lubridate)
  library(readr)
  library(stringr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(dplyr)
})

############################################################
# 1) Set working directory (analysis folder)
############################################################
setwd("/SynologyDrive/PhD/Projects/SpringBloom2021/DataAnalysis_SpringBloom2021/Supplementary/TransporterInvestment/Data_Filtration/")

############################################################
# 2) Load exported tables
#    (these should be created by your preprocessing script)
############################################################
abundance_table <- read_csv("../../../Figure_03/LakeOverview/Species_Abundance_Table.csv")
gene_table <- read_csv("../../../Figure_03/LakeOverview/Species_Gene_Table.csv")
protein_table <- read_csv("../../../Figure_03/LakeOverview/Species_Protein_Table.csv")
meta_table <- read_csv("../../../Figure_03/LakeOverview/Species_Meta_Table.csv")

############################################################
# 3) Quick inspection
############################################################
cat("=== Dimensions ===\n")
cat("species_abundance:", paste(dim(abundance_table), collapse = " x "), "\n")
cat("species_meta     :", paste(dim(meta_table), collapse = " x "), "\n")
cat("gene_table       :", paste(dim(gene_table), collapse = " x "), "\n")
cat("protein_table    :", paste(dim(protein_table), collapse = " x "), "\n\n")

cat("=== Glimpse ===\n")
glimpse(abundance_table)
glimpse(gene_table)
glimpse(protein_table)
glimpse(meta_table)

############################################################
# 4) Minimal key checks (optional but recommended)
############################################################
cat("\n=== Key checks ===\n")
cat("Unique species (abundance):", n_distinct(abundance_table$Species_ID), "\n")
cat("Unique species (meta):     ", n_distinct(meta_table$Species_ID), "\n")
cat("Unique species (protein):  ", n_distinct(protein_table$Species_ID), "\n")
cat("Unique dates (abundance):  ", n_distinct(abundance_table$Date), "\n")
cat("Unique dates (protein):    ", n_distinct(protein_table$Date), "\n")

############################################################
# 5) Keep only annotated proteins
############################################################
gene_table <- gene_table %>%
  filter(KEGG_ID != "Unclassified")

protein_table <- protein_table %>%
  filter(Protein_ID %in% gene_table$Protein_ID)
```

# highlight proteme and species filtering for analysis!
```{r}
############################################################
# Supplementary figure — retention of analysed trophic groups
# across downstream filtration steps
#
# Goal
#   Show how much of the original full dataset is retained after
#   successive downstream filtering steps used for transporter
#   investment analyses, separately for:
#     1) species
#     2) distinct proteins
#     3) total protein signal (summed IBAQ_Norm)
#
# What is shown
#   - Only the 3 downstream analysed steps are displayed:
#       1) Analysed species:
#          trophic index + species-specific, KEGG-annotated proteome
#       2) Valid observations:
#          >=10 proteins + transporter & non-transporter
#       3) Final dataset:
#          >=5 valid sampling dates
#
#   - Bar heights are percentages retained relative to the hidden
#     full reference dataset (= not plotted), allowing direct
#     comparison among species, proteins, and signal.
#
#   - Numbers above bars show absolute totals only.
#
# Grouping
#   - Only the three analysed trophic groups are shown:
#       Copiotroph, Intermediate, Oligotroph
#   - Stack order is fixed as:
#       bottom = Copiotroph
#       middle = Intermediate
#       top    = Oligotroph
#
# Important interpretation
#   - Species percentages are relative to the full species
#     universe in meta_table (e.g. 495 species).
#   - Protein percentages are relative to the hidden full
#     species-specific, KEGG-annotated detected proteome.
#   - Signal percentages are relative to the hidden full total
#     protein signal of that same full proteome reference.
#   - The hidden full reference is not plotted as a bar.
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(stringr)
  library(scales)
  library(patchwork)
})

############################################################
# 1) Plot settings
############################################################

# Stacking order: bottom -> top
plot_group_levels <- c("Copiotroph", "Intermediate", "Oligotroph")

plot_group_cols <- c(
  "Copiotroph"   = "#BA5205",
  "Intermediate" = "#EDEAE9",
  "Oligotroph"   = "#93C2E6"
)

theme_nat <- theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 9, colour = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

min_proteins_per_date <- 10
min_dates_per_species <- 5
groups_to_analyze <- c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like")

############################################################
# 2) Helper to recode only the plotted trophic groups
############################################################
recode_plot_groups <- function(x) {
  x <- str_trim(as.character(x))
  x[x == "Oligo_like"]   <- "Oligotroph"
  x[x == "Copio_like"]   <- "Copiotroph"
  x[x == "Intermediate"] <- "Intermediate"
  x
}

############################################################
# 3) Clean species metadata
#
# IMPORTANT:
#   Keep original grouping structure for computation.
#   Do NOT remove Filtered here.
############################################################
species_meta <- meta_table %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    Group_For_Plots = str_trim(as.character(Group_For_Plots)),
    Group_For_Plots = if_else(
      is.na(Group_For_Plots) | Group_For_Plots == "",
      "Filtered",
      Group_For_Plots
    ),
    Phylum = str_trim(as.character(Phylum)),
    Phylum = na_if(Phylum, "")
  )

############################################################
# 4) Define analysed species pool
############################################################
species_with_trophic <- species_meta %>%
  filter(
    TrophicInfo_Available %in% TRUE,
    Group_For_Plots %in% groups_to_analyze
  )

############################################################
# 5) Restrict gene table to KEGG-annotated proteins
############################################################
gene_table_kegg <- gene_table %>%
  filter(KEGG_ID != "Unclassified")

############################################################
# 6) Standardize grouping in protein_table
#
# IMPORTANT:
#   Keep original grouping structure for computation.
#   Do NOT remove Filtered here.
############################################################
protein_annotated <- protein_table %>%
  mutate(
    Group_For_Plots = str_trim(as.character(Group_For_Plots)),
    Group_For_Plots = if_else(
      is.na(Group_For_Plots) | Group_For_Plots == "",
      "Filtered",
      Group_For_Plots
    )
  )

############################################################
# 7) Build protein-level filtration objects
############################################################
prot_step0 <- protein_annotated

if ("Species_Specific_ProteinGroup" %in% names(prot_step0)) {
  prot_step0 <- prot_step0 %>%
    filter(Species_Specific_ProteinGroup %in% TRUE)
}

if ("any_missing_taxonomy" %in% names(prot_step0)) {
  prot_step0 <- prot_step0 %>%
    filter(!any_missing_taxonomy)
}

prot_step0 <- prot_step0 %>%
  filter(Protein_ID %in% gene_table_kegg$Protein_ID)

prot_step1 <- prot_step0 %>%
  filter(Species_ID %in% species_with_trophic$Species_ID)

############################################################
# 8) Transporter vs non-transporter classification
############################################################
protein_annotation <- gene_table_kegg %>%
  distinct(Protein_ID, KEGG_Subcategory) %>%
  mutate(
    Protein_Class = case_when(
      KEGG_Subcategory == "Transporters" ~ "Transporter",
      TRUE                               ~ "Non-transporter"
    )
  )

prot_step1_classified <- prot_step1 %>%
  left_join(protein_annotation, by = "Protein_ID") %>%
  mutate(
    Protein_Class = replace_na(Protein_Class, "Non-transporter")
  )

############################################################
# 9) Species-date validity filter
############################################################
sd_summary <- prot_step1_classified %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, Date, Protein_ID, Protein_Class) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    n_proteins = n_distinct(Protein_ID),
    transporter_n = n_distinct(Protein_ID[Protein_Class == "Transporter"]),
    nontransporter_n = n_distinct(Protein_ID[Protein_Class == "Non-transporter"]),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pass_valid_date = n_proteins >= min_proteins_per_date &
      transporter_n >= 1 &
      nontransporter_n >= 1
  )

keep_sd <- sd_summary %>%
  filter(pass_valid_date)

prot_step2 <- prot_step1_classified %>%
  semi_join(
    keep_sd %>% select(Species_ID, Date),
    by = c("Species_ID", "Date")
  )

############################################################
# 10) Species with >= 5 valid dates
############################################################
keep_species <- keep_sd %>%
  count(Species_ID, name = "n_valid_dates") %>%
  filter(n_valid_dates >= min_dates_per_species)

retained_species <- species_with_trophic %>%
  filter(Species_ID %in% keep_species$Species_ID)

prot_step3 <- prot_step2 %>%
  filter(Species_ID %in% retained_species$Species_ID)

############################################################
# 11) Step names
############################################################
step_names_all <- c(
  "Full dataset:\nKEGG-annotated, species-specific proteins",
  "Analysed species:\ntrophic index + species-specific\nKEGG-annotated proteome",
  "Valid observations:\n≥10 proteins + transporter & non-transporter",
  "Final dataset:\n≥5 valid sampling dates"
)

step_names_display <- step_names_all[2:4]

############################################################
# 12) Species counts across all 4 steps
#
# First compute EXACTLY as in the original 4-step figure.
############################################################
species_step0 <- species_meta %>%
  transmute(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots, step = step_names_all[1])

species_step1 <- species_with_trophic %>%
  transmute(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots, step = step_names_all[2])

species_step2 <- species_with_trophic %>%
  filter(Species_ID %in% keep_sd$Species_ID) %>%
  transmute(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots, step = step_names_all[3])

species_step3 <- retained_species %>%
  transmute(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots, step = step_names_all[4])

species_long_all <- bind_rows(
  species_step0, species_step1, species_step2, species_step3
)

species_counts_all <- species_long_all %>%
  count(step, Group_For_Plots, name = "value") %>%
  complete(
    step = step_names_all,
    Group_For_Plots = c("Intermediate", "Filtered", "Oligo_like", "Copio_like"),
    fill = list(value = 0)
  )

species_total_ref <- species_counts_all %>%
  filter(step == step_names_all[1]) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(value)) %>%
  pull(total)

species_totals <- species_counts_all %>%
  group_by(step) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(step %in% step_names_display)

# Plotting data only: remove Filtered, recode labels, keep denominator
species_plot_df <- species_counts_all %>%
  filter(step %in% step_names_display, Group_For_Plots != "Filtered") %>%
  mutate(
    Group_For_Plots = recode_plot_groups(Group_For_Plots),
    Group_For_Plots = factor(Group_For_Plots, levels = plot_group_levels),
    step = factor(step, levels = step_names_display),
    value_pct = 100 * value / species_total_ref
  )

############################################################
# 13) Protein-level step list
############################################################
protein_steps_list <- list(
  prot_step0,
  prot_step1,
  prot_step2,
  prot_step3
)
names(protein_steps_list) <- step_names_all

############################################################
# 14) Distinct protein counts across all 4 steps
#
# Again compute EXACTLY as in the original figure first.
############################################################
protein_count_all <- bind_rows(lapply(names(protein_steps_list), function(s) {
  protein_steps_list[[s]] %>%
    distinct(Protein_ID, Group_For_Plots) %>%
    mutate(step = s) %>%
    count(step, Group_For_Plots, name = "value")
})) %>%
  complete(
    step = step_names_all,
    Group_For_Plots = c("Intermediate", "Filtered", "Oligo_like", "Copio_like"),
    fill = list(value = 0)
  )

protein_total_ref <- protein_count_all %>%
  filter(step == step_names_all[1]) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(value)) %>%
  pull(total)

protein_count_totals <- protein_count_all %>%
  group_by(step) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(step %in% step_names_display)

# Plotting data only
protein_count_plot_df <- protein_count_all %>%
  filter(step %in% step_names_display, Group_For_Plots != "Filtered") %>%
  mutate(
    Group_For_Plots = recode_plot_groups(Group_For_Plots),
    Group_For_Plots = factor(Group_For_Plots, levels = plot_group_levels),
    step = factor(step, levels = step_names_display),
    value_pct = 100 * value / protein_total_ref
  )

############################################################
# 15) Total IBAQ signal across all 4 steps
#
# Again compute EXACTLY as in the original figure first.
############################################################
signal_all <- bind_rows(lapply(names(protein_steps_list), function(s) {
  protein_steps_list[[s]] %>%
    mutate(step = s) %>%
    group_by(step, Group_For_Plots) %>%
    summarise(
      value = sum(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
})) %>%
  complete(
    step = step_names_all,
    Group_For_Plots = c("Intermediate", "Filtered", "Oligo_like", "Copio_like"),
    fill = list(value = 0)
  )

signal_total_ref <- signal_all %>%
  filter(step == step_names_all[1]) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(value)) %>%
  pull(total)

signal_totals <- signal_all %>%
  group_by(step) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(step %in% step_names_display)

# Plotting data only
signal_plot_df <- signal_all %>%
  filter(step %in% step_names_display, Group_For_Plots != "Filtered") %>%
  mutate(
    Group_For_Plots = recode_plot_groups(Group_For_Plots),
    Group_For_Plots = factor(Group_For_Plots, levels = plot_group_levels),
    step = factor(step, levels = step_names_display),
    value_pct = 100 * value / signal_total_ref
  )

############################################################
# 16) Species plot
############################################################
p_species <- ggplot(
  species_plot_df,
  aes(x = step, y = value_pct, fill = Group_For_Plots)
) +
  geom_col(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3, width = 0.78) +
  geom_text(
    data = species_totals,
    aes(
      x = factor(step, levels = step_names_display),
      y = 100 * total / species_total_ref + 3,
      label = total
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3.0,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = plot_group_cols,
    limits = plot_group_levels,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Species retained [%]"
  ) +
  theme_nat

############################################################
# 17) Protein plot
############################################################
p_proteins <- ggplot(
  protein_count_plot_df,
  aes(x = step, y = value_pct, fill = Group_For_Plots)
) +
  geom_col(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3, width = 0.78) +
  geom_text(
    data = protein_count_totals,
    aes(
      x = factor(step, levels = step_names_display),
      y = 100 * total / protein_total_ref + 3,
      label = comma(total)
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3.0,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = plot_group_cols,
    limits = plot_group_levels,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proteins retained [%]"
  ) +
  theme_nat +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

############################################################
# 18) Signal plot
############################################################
p_signal <- ggplot(
  signal_plot_df,
  aes(x = step, y = value_pct, fill = Group_For_Plots)
) +
  geom_col(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3, width = 0.78) +
  geom_text(
    data = signal_totals,
    aes(
      x = factor(step, levels = step_names_display),
      y = 100 * total / signal_total_ref + 3,
      label = label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale())(total)
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3.0,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = plot_group_cols,
    limits = plot_group_levels,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Protein signal retained [%]"
  ) +
  theme_nat +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

############################################################
# 19) Assemble final figure
############################################################
final_filtration_overview <- p_species + p_proteins + p_signal +
  plot_layout(ncol = 3, guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "top")

print(final_filtration_overview)

############################################################
# 20) Save figure
############################################################
ggsave(
  filename = "Supplementary_Filtration_Overview_Percent_3steps_row.png",
  plot = final_filtration_overview,
  width = 15,
  height = 5.6,
  dpi = 600,
  bg = "white"
)
```








# temporal coverage reateind after filtering used for tpf and bc calculations
```{r}
############################################################
# Supplementary figure — temporal coverage after filtration
#
# What this figure shows
#   a) Distribution of valid dates per species in the analysed
#      trophic-index-defined pool. A valid date is one where a
#      species has at least 10 detected proteins, including at
#      least one transporter and one non-transporter.
#
#   b) Longest run of consecutive valid sampling dates per final
#      retained species, coloured by trophic group. Consecutive
#      refers to adjacent sampling occasions in the ordered
#      proteomics time series, not uninterrupted calendar days.
#
# Figure layout
#   - Two panels in one row
#   - Left: valid dates per species
#   - Right: longest consecutive valid-date runs
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(stringr)
  library(scales)
  library(patchwork)
})

############################################################
# 1) User-defined thresholds and plotting settings
############################################################
min_proteins_per_date <- 10
min_dates_per_species <- 5
min_consecutive_dates <- 4
groups_to_analyze <- c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like")

trophic_levels <- c("Oligotroph", "Intermediate", "Copiotroph")

trophic_cols <- c(
  "Oligotroph"   = "#93C2E6",
  "Intermediate" = "#EDEAE9",
  "Copiotroph"   = "#BA5205"
)

theme_nat <- theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10, colour = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 9, colour = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

############################################################
# 2) Canonical species metadata
#
# Restrict to one row per species and define the three analysed
# trophic groups used downstream.
############################################################
species_meta <- meta_table %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    Group_For_Plots = str_trim(as.character(Group_For_Plots)),
    Group_For_Plots = na_if(Group_For_Plots, ""),
    TrophicGroup_clean = case_when(
      Group_For_Plots == "Oligo_like"   ~ "Oligotroph",
      Group_For_Plots == "Intermediate" ~ "Intermediate",
      Group_For_Plots == "Copio_like"   ~ "Copiotroph",
      TRUE                              ~ NA_character_
    ),
    TrophicGroup_clean = factor(TrophicGroup_clean, levels = trophic_levels)
  )

############################################################
# 3) Define the analysed starting pool
#
# These are species with trophic information and assignment to
# one of the three downstream trophic groups.
############################################################
species_with_trophic <- species_meta %>%
  filter(
    TrophicInfo_Available %in% TRUE,
    Group_For_Plots %in% groups_to_analyze,
    !is.na(TrophicGroup_clean)
  )

############################################################
# 4) Reproduce the protein-level prefiltering used before
#    date-level structural filtering
############################################################
prot_base <- protein_table %>%
  filter(Group_For_Plots %in% groups_to_analyze)

if ("Species_Specific_ProteinGroup" %in% names(prot_base)) {
  prot_base <- prot_base %>%
    filter(Species_Specific_ProteinGroup %in% TRUE)
}

if ("any_missing_taxonomy" %in% names(prot_base)) {
  prot_base <- prot_base %>%
    filter(!any_missing_taxonomy)
}

prot_base <- prot_base %>%
  filter(Species_ID %in% species_with_trophic$Species_ID)

############################################################
# 5) Annotate proteins as transporter or non-transporter
############################################################
protein_annotation <- gene_table %>%
  filter(KEGG_ID != "Unclassified") %>%
  distinct(Protein_ID, KEGG_Subcategory) %>%
  mutate(
    Protein_Class = case_when(
      KEGG_Subcategory == "Transporters" ~ "Transporter",
      TRUE                               ~ "Non-transporter"
    )
  )

prot_base <- prot_base %>%
  left_join(protein_annotation, by = "Protein_ID") %>%
  mutate(
    Protein_Class = replace_na(Protein_Class, "Non-transporter")
  )

############################################################
# 6) Summarise each species-date and define valid dates
#
# A valid date requires:
#   - >= 10 detected proteins
#   - >= 1 transporter
#   - >= 1 non-transporter
############################################################
sd_summary <- prot_base %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, Date, Protein_ID, Protein_Class) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    n_proteins = n_distinct(Protein_ID),
    transporter_equiv = n_distinct(Protein_ID[Protein_Class == "Transporter"]),
    nontransporter_equiv = n_distinct(Protein_ID[Protein_Class == "Non-transporter"]),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Pass_Structural = transporter_equiv >= 1 & nontransporter_equiv >= 1,
    Pass_Coverage10 = Pass_Structural & (n_proteins >= min_proteins_per_date)
  )

keep_sd <- sd_summary %>%
  filter(Pass_Coverage10)

############################################################
# 7) Final retained species: >= 5 valid dates
############################################################
keep_species <- keep_sd %>%
  count(Species_ID, name = "n_dates") %>%
  filter(n_dates >= min_dates_per_species)

retained_species <- species_with_trophic %>%
  filter(Species_ID %in% keep_species$Species_ID) %>%
  left_join(keep_species, by = "Species_ID")

############################################################
# 8) Panel a data — distribution of valid dates per species
#
# Species with zero valid dates remain represented at zero.
############################################################
dates_distribution <- species_with_trophic %>%
  select(Species_ID, TrophicGroup_clean) %>%
  left_join(
    keep_sd %>% count(Species_ID, name = "n_qualifying_dates"),
    by = "Species_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    n_qualifying_dates = replace_na(n_qualifying_dates, 0L),
    TrophicGroup_clean = factor(as.character(TrophicGroup_clean), levels = trophic_levels)
  ) %>%
  count(n_qualifying_dates, TrophicGroup_clean, name = "n") %>%
  complete(
    n_qualifying_dates = 0:max(36, max(n_qualifying_dates, na.rm = TRUE)),
    TrophicGroup_clean = factor(trophic_levels, levels = trophic_levels),
    fill = list(n = 0)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    TrophicGroup_clean = factor(as.character(TrophicGroup_clean), levels = trophic_levels)
  )

############################################################
# 9) Panel b data — longest run of consecutive valid dates
#
# Consecutive means adjacent sampling occasions in the ordered
# proteomics date series.
############################################################
sampling_dates <- sort(unique(as.Date(protein_table$Date)))

valid_dates_retained <- keep_sd %>%
  filter(Species_ID %in% retained_species$Species_ID) %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date)) %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  mutate(date_index = match(Date, sampling_dates)) %>%
  arrange(Species_ID, date_index)

consecutive_summary <- valid_dates_retained %>%
  group_by(Species_ID) %>%
  arrange(date_index, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    new_run = c(TRUE, diff(date_index) != 1),
    run_id  = cumsum(new_run)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, run_id) %>%
  summarise(
    run_length = n(),
    start_date = min(Date),
    end_date   = max(Date),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    longest_consecutive_run = max(run_length),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    retained_species %>%
      distinct(Species_ID, TrophicGroup_clean),
    by = "Species_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    TrophicGroup_clean = factor(as.character(TrophicGroup_clean), levels = trophic_levels)
  )

############################################################
# 10) Console summaries
############################################################
cat("\n====================================================\n")
cat("VALID DATES PER SPECIES — ANALYSED STARTING POOL\n")
cat("====================================================\n")
print(
  species_with_trophic %>%
    left_join(
      keep_sd %>% count(Species_ID, name = "n_valid_dates"),
      by = "Species_ID"
    ) %>%
    mutate(n_valid_dates = replace_na(n_valid_dates, 0L)) %>%
    summarise(
      n_species = n(),
      min_valid_dates = min(n_valid_dates),
      median_valid_dates = median(n_valid_dates),
      mean_valid_dates = mean(n_valid_dates),
      max_valid_dates = max(n_valid_dates)
    )
)

cat("\n====================================================\n")
cat("LONGEST CONSECUTIVE VALID-DATE RUN PER RETAINED SPECIES\n")
cat("====================================================\n")
print(
  consecutive_summary %>%
    group_by(TrophicGroup_clean) %>%
    summarise(
      n_species = n(),
      min_run = min(longest_consecutive_run),
      median_run = median(longest_consecutive_run),
      mean_run = mean(longest_consecutive_run),
      max_run = max(longest_consecutive_run),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
)

############################################################
# 11) Panel a — distribution of valid dates per species
############################################################
dates_totals <- dates_distribution %>%
  group_by(n_qualifying_dates) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(n), .groups = "drop")

p_a <- ggplot(
  dates_distribution,
  aes(x = n_qualifying_dates, y = n, fill = TrophicGroup_clean)
) +
  geom_col(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3, width = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = min_dates_per_species - 0.5,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = min_dates_per_species + 1.1,
    y = max(dates_totals$total) * 0.95,
    label = paste0("cutoff = ", min_dates_per_species, " dates"),
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = trophic_cols,
    limits = trophic_levels,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = pretty_breaks(n = 10),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.01))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = pretty_breaks(n = 6),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("Dates with >= ", min_proteins_per_date, " detected proteins"),
    y = "Species [n]",
    title = "a"
  ) +
  theme_nat +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)
  )

############################################################
# 12) Panel b — longest consecutive valid-date runs
#
# A dashed line marks the cutoff of 4 consecutive valid dates.
############################################################
run_totals <- consecutive_summary %>%
  count(longest_consecutive_run, name = "total")

p_b <- ggplot(
  consecutive_summary,
  aes(x = longest_consecutive_run, fill = TrophicGroup_clean)
) +
  geom_histogram(
    binwidth = 1,
    boundary = 0.5,
    colour = "black",
    position = "stack"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = min_consecutive_dates - 0.5,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = min_consecutive_dates + 1.1,
    y = max(run_totals$total) * 0.95,
    label = paste0("cutoff = ", min_consecutive_dates, " dates"),
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = trophic_cols,
    limits = trophic_levels,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      2,
      max(consecutive_summary$longest_consecutive_run, na.rm = TRUE),
      by = 2
    ),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = pretty_breaks(n = 6),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Longest run of consecutive valid dates",
    y = "Species [n]",
    title = "b"
  ) +
  theme_nat +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)
  )

############################################################
# 13) Assemble final 2-panel figure
#
# One row:
#   a, b
############################################################
final_temporal_coverage_figure <- p_a | p_b

print(final_temporal_coverage_figure)

############################################################
# 14) Save final figure
############################################################
ggsave(
  filename = "Supplementary_ValidDates_and_ConsecutiveRuns_AB.png",
  plot = final_temporal_coverage_figure,
  width = 12,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 600,
  bg = "white"
)
```
